label: ponder: research
prompt:
You are running a ponder session for the idea 'research' in the sdlc workspace.

The owner (jordan) has seeded this session with the following message — include it verbatim at the top of your session log as `**jordan · Owner**\nresearch

our knowledge page at /knowledge needs a way to be able to issues research jobs that are very similar to the evolve job found at ~/Workspace/orchard9/adk-go-researcher`:

> research

our knowledge page at /knowledge needs a way to be able to issues research jobs that are very similar to the evolve job found at ~/Workspace/orchard9/adk-go-researcher

## Step 1 — Load context

```bash
sdlc ponder show research
sdlc ponder session list research
```

Read the manifest, every scrapbook artifact, and the most recent session log (`sdlc ponder session read research <N>`) to restore full context. Load team member agent definitions from .claude/agents/. Orient from the orientation strip (WHERE WE ARE / NEXT MOVE / COMMIT SIGNAL).

## Step 2 — Run the session

You are a facilitator. Channel recruited thought partners by name — voice their perspectives, let them push back, surface tensions. Interrogate the brief: push past stated solutions to find real problems. Capture insights as scrapbook artifacts:
```bash
sdlc ponder capture research --content "<markdown>" --as <name>.md
```
Use inline markers: `⚑  Decided:` for resolved points, `?  Open:` for live tensions.

## Step 3 — Log the session (MANDATORY)

Before ending, you MUST log the session. This is not optional — skipping it means the session is invisible to the web UI and to future agents.

The ONLY correct procedure:
1. Write the complete session Markdown to a temp file using the Write tool:
`/tmp/ponder-session-research.md`
2. Run: `sdlc ponder session log research --file /tmp/ponder-session-research.md`

This command auto-numbers the file, places it in the `sessions/` subdirectory, increments the session counter, and mirrors orientation to the manifest.
